name: Carrizosa from Villanueva de los Infantes – circular via Santa Cruz de los Cáñamos and Castillo de Montiel
name_original: Carrizosa desde Villanueva de los Infantes – circular por Santa Cruz de los Cáñamos y Castillo de Montiel
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villanueva De Los Infantes traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains, interspersed with historical sites. The region features moderate elevation changes, with routes often connecting small towns and points of interest. Road cycling in this area offers paths through open countryside and past local landmarks. Best road cycling routes around Villanueva De Los Infantes The most popular road cycling route is Albaladejo and Montiel Castles Loop, a 45.5 miles (73.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects historical castles and offers views of the surrounding rural landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Carrizosa from Villanueva de los Infantes – circular via Santa Cruz de los Cáñamos and Castillo de Montiel, a moderate 43.9 miles (70.7 km) path. This circular route passes through several villages and features a castle. Local road cyclists also love the Torrenueva from Cózar – circular via Almedina and Torre de Juan Abad, a 56.8 miles (91.4 km) trail leading through rural villages and open fields, often completed in about 3 hours 53 minutes. Road cycling around Villanueva De Los Infantes is defined by undulating terrain, historical landmarks, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including several moderate routes. The routes in Villanueva De Los Infantes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 40 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Los Infantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villanueva De Los Infantes?
A: There are 7 dedicated road cycling routes around Villanueva De Los Infantes, offering a variety of experiences through the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in the area?
A: While the routes are generally moderate, the area's terrain of rolling hills and agricultural plains makes it accessible. For those looking for a slightly shorter, moderate option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montiel – Ermita del Cristo de Jamila loop from Montiel&lt;/a&gt; is a good choice at 27.8 miles (44.8 km).

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of road cycling routes near Villanueva De Los Infantes?
A: The road cycling routes in this region typically range from about 27.8 miles (44.8 km) to 56.8 miles (91.4 km). Most moderate routes can be completed in approximately 3 to 4 hours, depending on your pace.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086137267/carrizosa-from-villanueva-de-los-infantes-circular-via-santa-cruz-de-los-canamos-and-castillo-de-montiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrizosa from Villanueva de los Infantes – circular via Santa Cruz de los Cáñamos and Castillo de Montiel&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086164982/torrenueva-from-cozar-circular-via-almedina-and-torre-de-juan-abad" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrenueva from Cózar – circular via Almedina and Torre de Juan Abad&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on these road cycling routes?
A: The routes traverse a landscape of rolling hills and agricultural plains, often connecting small towns and historical sites. You'll experience open countryside views and pass by local landmarks, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085974301/albaladejo-and-montiel-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albaladejo and Montiel Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt; connects historical castles. You might also encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4051640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4051610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convent of Santo Domingo&lt;/a&gt; in Villanueva De Los Infantes itself.

Q: Are there any routes that feature natural attractions like waterfalls?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31865414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hundimiento Waterfall – Laguna del Rey loop from Carrizosa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that includes a waterfall, offering a scenic natural highlight during your ride.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Villanueva De Los Infantes?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn are generally ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters are typically mild but can be cooler.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Villanueva De Los Infantes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the undulating terrain, the historical landmarks encountered along the way, and the peaceful agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there options for more advanced road cyclists?
A: While most routes are moderate, the longer distances and cumulative elevation gain on some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086164982/torrenueva-from-cozar-circular-via-almedina-and-torre-de-juan-abad" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrenueva from Cózar – circular via Almedina and Torre de Juan Abad&lt;/a&gt; at 56.8 miles (91.4 km), can provide a good challenge for more experienced riders.

Q: Is parking available near the starting points of these routes?
A: Villanueva De Los Infantes and the surrounding towns typically offer public parking options. It is advisable to check local parking availability in the specific village or town where you plan to start your ride.
tips:
Santa Cruz de los Cáñamos is a small town in Campo de Montiel (Ciudad Real), ideal for those seeking history, nature, and tranquility.
Its roots date back to the Middle Ages, with Roman and early Christian remains on the Torrejones hill (Monte Santo). The name comes from the historical cultivation of hemp, which was very important to the local economy.
Parish Church of San Bartolomé: Built in the 15th century with materials from the old castle. Its unique nave, coffered vault, and altarpiece of the Most Holy Christ of Inspiration are notable.

Montiel Castle, also known as the Star Castle, is one of the most important historical sites in Castile-La Mancha.
Built by the Arabs in the 9th century, under the name of Munt-Yil, it was part of the defense of the Islamic territory against the Christian advance.
It was conquered by the Christians in 1227, after a long siege, and passed into the hands of the Order of Santiago, who made it the center of the Commandery of Campo de Montiel.
The Tragedy of Montiel (1369) occurred here, where King Peter I of Castile was assassinated by his brother Henry of Trastámara, with Bertrand Duguesclin famously saying: "I neither remove nor appoint a king, but I help my lord."
It is currently in a state of partial ruin, but excavations and guided tours are taking place.

It is located in La Carrasca, about 14 km north of Villahermosa. According to tradition, the Virgin Mary appeared to a shepherd named Juan Cortés on top of an oak tree, from which the name of the sanctuary comes. A hermitage was built on the site of the apparition, which over time became the current sanctuary.
Beautiful town that is located in what is called the Campo de Montiel, a large extension within the same bellow. The locality is crossed by the Río Cañamares and which goes west, towards Damiel.

The Plaza Mayor of Villanueva de los Infantes is considered one of the most beautiful main squares in Castilla-La Mancha, not in vain has it been declared a Historic-Artistic Site, with notable buildings such as the Town Hall, emblazoned mansions, porticoed gallery, the Hospital de Santiago, the Convent of Santo Domingo or the Church of San Andrés Apóstol.
